what_it_does: A security-fix migration (labeled C-1, an adversarial-review CRITICAL fix) that drops the broad placeholder using(true) SELECT policies shipped in 0003/0004 on channels, channel_posts, dna_tokens, design_seeds, and design_submissions, revokes anon SELECT on the shared tables, and adds a DO-block sanity check that raises an exception if any of the named broad using(true) policies still remain.
observations: Explains the root issue: Postgres ORs all matching permissive policies, so a broad using(true) policy OR-widens past the scoped 0005/0006 policies, leaking every channel/post/submission to design_clients and anyone with the anon key.
Drops named policies idempotently (drop policy if exists) on five tables.
Revokes SELECT on channels, channel_posts, dna_tokens, design_seeds, design_submissions from anon.
Final DO $$ block queries pg_policies for the named policies with qual = 'true' and raises 'C-1: broad using(true) SELECT policy still present' if any remain.
Notes 0003/0004 source were also fixed; this migration is belt-and-suspenders for already-applied environments including a fresh db reset.
standouts: The OR-widening insight — permissive RLS policies are OR-combined, so a leftover using(true) silently defeats all scoped policies.
The self-verifying DO block that fails the migration loudly if the broad policies survive.
conventions: Inline root-cause commentary tying the change to an adversarial review finding (C-1).
Fail-loud assertion at the end (raise exception on residual bad state).
concerns: The sanity check only inspects a hardcoded list of policy names; a differently-named broad using(true) policy on these tables would not be caught.
notes: This removes the accidental using(true) read-everything backdoor so the scoped 0005/0006 policies become authoritative, with a built-in assertion that the leak is gone.
questions: Are there any other tables in this schema that might still carry a broad using(true) SELECT policy outside this named list?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — header states it drops broad read policies and revokes anon read; the statements do exactly that plus self-verify.
apparent_themes: Least privilege (scoped policies authoritative, anon gets nothing)
Fail-loud verification baked into the migration
intention_signals: Trying to close a critical RLS read-leak by removing placeholder broad policies and proving, within the migration, that no leak remains.
novelty: An in-migration pg_policies assertion that raises if the very policies it dropped are still present — verification co-located with the fix.
contribution: Contributes the C-1 leak remediation: removal of broad using(true) read policies, anon SELECT revocation, and the self-verifying residual-policy check.
summary_for_embedding: A Supabase migration that drops broad using(true) SELECT policies and revokes anon read on the design shared tables, with a DO-block assertion that no broad policy remains.
intention_for_embedding: To fix a critical RLS read-leak where permissive broad policies OR-widened past the scoped policies, and to verify the fix held.
